level_2.origins_history:
Muscat of Alexandria is one of the oldest and most widely planted grape varieties in the world, used for both wine and table grapes. DNA analysis confirms it is an offspring of Muscat Blanc à Petits Grains, likely originating in North Africa or the eastern Mediterranean.

The variety takes its name from the Egyptian city of Alexandria, a major trading hub of the ancient world. It spread throughout the Mediterranean and was carried to the New World by Spanish colonizers, where it became established in South America and California.

Muscat of Alexandria produces wines ranging from dry to very sweet, including Spain's Moscatel dessert wines and Australia's fortified styles. The variety's large berries also make it popular as a table grape, sold as Hanepoot in South Africa and Zibibbo in Sicily.

genetic_lineage.overview: Muscat of Alexandria is an offspring of Muscat Blanc à Petits Grains.

parents.name: Muscat Blanc à Petits Grains
parents.notes: Confirmed parent. Cross with Axina de Tres Bias.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: As both a parent of many varieties and one of the most widely planted grapes globally, Muscat of Alexandria is foundational to world viticulture.

clones.name: Zibibbo Selection
clones.characteristics: Traditional Sicilian selections. Used for both wine and dried grapes (Passito).

clones.name: Moscatel Selection
clones.characteristics: Spanish selections for dessert wine production. High sugar accumulation.

terroir.effect: Found on Pantelleria. Produces intensely concentrated Passito wines.

wine_character.aromatics: Dried apricot, orange peel, honey, volcanic mineral

terroir.effect: Common in Spanish regions. Produces aromatic sweet wines.
Found in various New World regions. Produces lighter, table grape-like wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Large berries can be affected in humid conditions.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Sometimes used beneficially for sweet wine production

susceptibilities.notes: Susceptible to powdery mildew like other Muscats.

disease_profile.general_notes: Muscat of Alexandria is a vigorous, late-ripening variety that produces large berries suitable for both wine and table use. The variety develops very high sugar levels in hot climates, making it ideal for sweet and fortified wines. It is more heat-tolerant than Muscat Blanc à Petits Grains.

regional_expressions.style_summary: Italy's most prized Muscat of Alexandria (Zibibbo) comes from this windswept volcanic island between Sicily and Tunisia. Passito di Pantelleria is made from sun-dried grapes, producing intensely sweet, concentrated wines. The alberello (bush vine) training protects vines from constant winds.

typical_character.aromatics: Dried apricot, orange peel, honey, volcanic mineral, herbs

regional_expressions.style_summary: Historic Spanish region producing sweet Moscatel wines. Styles range from young and fresh to aged and oxidative. The region's reputation has declined from its 19th-century heights but quality producers are reviving traditional styles.

typical_character.aromatics: Grape, orange, honey, raisin, caramel (aged styles)
